title: Armoire with Drawers and Mirrored Door
summary: A tall plywood armoire with a mirrored door on one side, an open shelved compartment on the other, and four drawers below — built entirely with pocket-hole plywood casework.
description:
This is an advanced, multi-session build: a full case of plywood ripped into 15-3/4" strips (referred to as "1x16" throughout the cut list), open 2x2 leg construction, three small drawers plus one large drawer, a mirrored door, and a face-frame trim system that has to be built to the actual mirror and drawer-face dimensions on hand — not just the numbers in the cut list. Drill every pocket hole for the whole project before you start assembling anything; the plan is written assuming holes are ready when each step needs them, and going back to drill into an assembled case is far harder than doing it on flat stock.

The drawer faces are sized for 9-1/4" wide fronts with a 1/8" gap on all sides — build the drawer boxes themselves about 1" narrower than their openings to leave room for the slides, and don't attach any drawer or door face until the case itself is fully trimmed out, since the trim is what the faces register against for that even reveal. This armoire can also be built 24" deep, or any depth, by changing the rip width of the plywood — for a narrower cabinet, 1x12s can stand in for the sides.

materials.name: 3/4" plywood, ripped 15-3/4" strips, 8 ft (2 sheets)
materials.note: Referred to as "1x16" throughout the plan.

materials.name: 16" Euro-style bottom corner mount drawer slides, pair

steps.title: Cut all the parts
steps.body: Cut two sides at 67", one bottom shelf at 44", one center divider at 56", one upper shelf at 23-1/4" and one top panel at 49" from the 3/4" plywood, ripped 15-3/4" strips, 8 ft (2 sheets). Cut four side trim, top and bottom at 15-3/4", one top trim, 1x3 at 49", two door stiles at 54-1/4", two door top rails at 14", two drawer face frame stiles at 23-1/2" and two drawer face frame rails at 18" from the Pine 1x3. Cut four legs at 69", two top supports at 44" and two center divider trim at 54-1/2" from the Pine 2x2. Cut four bottom drawer trim at 44", four smaller drawer trim at 23-1/4" and one top trim, 1x2 at 49" from the Pine 1x2. Cut six small drawer sides at 16", six small drawer front/back at 20-3/4", two large drawer sides at 41-1/2" and two large drawer front/back at 16" from the Pine 1x8. Cut three small drawer bottoms at 22-1/4" and one large drawer bottom at 43" from the 1/4" plywood or hardboard, 2 sheets. Cut three small drawer face panels at 23" and one large drawer face panel at 43-3/4" from the Pine 1x10. Cut one door mirror backer panel at 54-1/4" from the 1/4" plywood or hardboard, 2 sheets. Cut one small drawer face backer panel at 23-1/2" from the 1/4" plywood or hardboard, 2 sheets. Allowing about 1/8" for each saw cut, that comes to four 8 ft Pine 1x3, seven 8 ft Pine 2x2, four 8 ft Pine 1x2, four 8 ft Pine 1x8 and one 10 ft Pine 1x10. Square every cut and label each piece as it comes off the saw — several parts here are within half an inch of each other and are easy to mix up later.

steps.title: Drill pocket holes and build the sides
steps.body: This armoire is built to a specific mirror and to drawer faces ripped from 1x10 at 9-1/4" wide, so read the whole plan before cutting into it. Drill every pocket hole for the entire project now, while the stock is still flat — going back to drill inside an assembled 67"-tall case is far harder than doing it on the bench. Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the side edges of both 67" side panels, then attach the four 15-3/4" 1x3 side trim pieces across the top and bottom of each panel, flush with the panel edges.

steps.title: Attach the legs
steps.body: Attach the legs to the sides with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws through the predrilled holes, keeping the inside edges flush. Drill 3/4" pocket holes along the top edges, pointing upward on the inside, for attaching the top later.

steps.title: Join the sides with the top supports
steps.body: Stand the two side assemblies parallel and join them with the two 44" 2x2 top supports, one at the front and one at the back, running between the sides at the top. That 44" span is the case's interior width and every part above it depends on it — the bottom shelf, the bottom drawer trim and the large drawer face are all cut to it. Check both diagonals across the open case and clamp it square before driving the screws.

steps.title: Add the bottom shelf
steps.body: The 44" bottom shelf spans between the two sides and is what divides the case: everything above it is the shelved and mirrored compartments, and the 11" of case left below it is the large drawer bay. Position it so the 56" center divider standing on top of it reaches the top of the 67" sides, then fasten it through pocket holes into both side panels. Before it goes in, drill 3/4" pocket holes along its front and back edges pointing outward — those are for the bottom drawer trim in the next step, and you cannot reach them once the shelf is captured.

steps.title: Trim the bottom shelf
steps.body: The four 44" 1x2 bottom drawer trims frame the large drawer bay: two on the front, above and below the opening, and two matching them on the back. Attach them through the pocket holes you drilled in the shelf edges with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, plus glue and 1-1/4" brad nails where a screw cannot reach. Keep the front pair perfectly parallel — they are what the 43-3/4" drawer face registers its 1/8" reveal against, and any taper in the opening shows up as a wedge-shaped gap.

steps.title: Install the center divider and its trim
steps.body: Stand the 56" center divider panel on the bottom shelf, running front to back, and fasten it up through pocket holes into the shelf and into the top support. It does not sit centered: the 44" interior less the 1-1/2" 2x2 divider trim leaves 23-1/4" on one side for the shelved and drawered compartment and 19-1/4" on the other for the mirrored door, and those two figures are what the 23-1/4" upper shelf and the 19"-wide door are cut to. Then add the two 54-1/2" 2x2 center divider trims to the divider's front and back edges, keeping them flush to the inside of the larger drawer compartment — that flush face is the reference the drawer trim and faces all measure from.

steps.title: Add the smaller drawer trim
steps.body: The four 23-1/4" 1x2 smaller drawer trims divide the 23-1/4"-wide compartment into the three small drawer openings, two on the front and two matching on the back. Space them so each opening suits a 9-1/4"-wide drawer face with a 1/8" gap on all sides — that is a 9-1/2" clear opening per drawer. Attach with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws and check each opening's width at both ends before moving on; the boxes in a later step are built 1" narrower than the opening and there is no slack to absorb a taper.

steps.title: Add the upper shelf
steps.body: The 23-1/4" upper shelf closes the top of the drawered compartment and forms the floor of the open shelved cubby above it. Drill 3/4" pocket holes on all four edges of the shelf, then set it level between the side panel and the center divider and screw it home. Its length matches the compartment exactly, so dry-fit it before driving anything — if it fights, the divider is out of plumb rather than the shelf being long.

steps.title: Build and attach the top
steps.body: Build the top panel first, trimming out the sides with the 1x2 and 1x3, then attach the assembled top to the case.

steps.title: Build and install the drawers
steps.body: Build four boxes: three small ones from the 16" sides and 20-3/4" front and back pieces, and one large one from the 41-1/2" sides and 16" front and back pieces. In every box the 16" pieces run the full length and the other pair fits between them, which the 1/4" bottoms confirm — a small box lands at 22-1/4" outside (20-3/4" plus two 3/4" ends) to match its 22-1/4" bottom, and the large box at 43" to match its 43" bottom. That leaves exactly 1" in the 23-1/4" small openings and in the 44" large opening, which is the clearance the Euro slides need. Keep the pocket holes on the outside faces where the drawer face will hide them, and mount the boxes inset 3/4" back from the front of the case so the faces sit proud.
materials: 16" Euro-style bottom corner mount drawer slides, pair

steps.title: Build and install the mirrored door
steps.body: The door frame closes exactly on the mirror, so build it to the numbers: two 54-1/4" 1x3 stiles with the two 14" 1x3 rails between them gives a door 19" wide by 54-1/4" tall — matching the 19" x 54-1/4" backer panel — and the field left inside is 14" wide by 49-1/4" tall, which is the mirror. Join the frame with 3/4" pocket holes and 1-1/4" screws, then pin the 1/4" backer to the back of the frame with 3/4" brad nails. Hang the door on the narrow profile hinges in the 19-1/4" opening and set the mirror into the field only after the door swings true.

steps.title: Attach the drawer faces
steps.body: The faces are sized so the reveal falls out on its own: each 23" small face in a 23-1/4" opening and the 43-3/4" large face in the 44" opening both leave 1/8" all round. Center each face over its closed drawer box on 1/8" spacers, then fix it with glue and 1-1/4" brad nails driven from inside the box — the plan's fastener here is a brad, not a finish nail. Finally drill for and fit the knobs or handles on the four faces and the mirrored door.

images.alt: A tall plywood armoire with a mirrored door, open shelving, and four drawers
